title: grants — HTTP middleware
description: One middleware this module contributes: `grants.warm_grant_cache`. Deferred per-request cache warming so the first policy check on a tenant request already has the caller's grant footprint in L1. Also relies on framework + tenants middleware composed on its route groups.

middleware.description: On authenticated Sanctum requests, pre-loads the caller's active grants into the L1 (in-process) evaluation cache. Runs AFTER auth + tenant resolution but BEFORE the first policy check. Zero-latency for requests that touch no grantable resources (skipped when the auth user has no active grants — checked via a fast COUNT on the composite index). For requests where grants apply, warming makes the first check a cache-hit instead of a DB round-trip.
sideEffects: Reads active grants WHERE (tenant_id = caller.tenant, subject_id = caller.id, revoked_at IS NULL). Ceiling of 500 grants per request (settings.grants.warm_cache_ceiling); beyond that, caching happens lazily on first check.
Populates the L1 GrantEvaluationCache keyed by (tenant, subject, resource_type, resource_id).
Emits GrantEvaluationCacheHit / GrantEvaluationCacheMiss telemetry (sampled) for observability.
For delegated callers (see access/delegation), ALSO pre-loads the delegator's grants so the union query is fast.

notes: The warm-cache middleware is the ONLY grant-specific HTTP middleware in Wave 1b. Cache invalidation lives in the model observer + the InvalidateGrantCacheJob — not in middleware.

For platform-admin routes, warming is skipped — platform admins never carry per-resource grants (they operate on the platform_admin guard, which has no grant footprint). The middleware detects this and short-circuits.

For high-cardinality principals (a support user granted 5000+ grants — unusual but possible for a compliance officer reviewing a large tenant), the middleware caps at 500 and delegates the rest to lazy L2 Redis lookups. Metric `academorix.grants.warm_cache.overflow.total` tracks these.
Feature-flag `grants.cache-warm` can turn the middleware off in an incident without a redeploy. When off, resolver falls back to per-check DB lookups (slower but correct).
